diff --git a/app/api/routes/pipeline.py b/app/api/routes/pipeline.py
index a1b2c3d..e5f6g7h 100644
--- a/app/api/routes/pipeline.py
+++ b/app/api/routes/pipeline.py
@@ -23,10 +23,14 @@
 
 @router.post("/run", status_code=202)
 async def start_run(
     body: PipelineRunRequest,
     background_tasks: BackgroundTasks,
 ) -> dict:
     """
     Start a new pipeline run in the background.
     """
+    # Input Validation Guard
+    if not body.diff_text or len(body.diff_text.strip()) < 10:
+        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="diff_text must be at least 10 characters long.")
+        
     orchestrator = PipelineOrchestrator()
     # A temporary orchestration is needed to get the run_id before starting

scenarios.title: Successfully trigger a new pipeline run via API
scenarios.description: Verify that a POST request to /api/pipeline/run initiates a background task and returns a valid run_id.
preconditions: API service is running and accessible

steps.assertion: Response body contains 'run_id' and 'status' set to 'queued'

scenarios.expected_result: A 202 Accepted status is returned with a unique UUID representing the pipeline execution.

scenarios.title: Verify reports list reflects new pipeline run entry
scenarios.description: Ensure that once a run is triggered, it appears in the reports list summary.
preconditions: A pipeline run has been successfully triggered

steps.assertion: The list contains the run_id generated in the previous step

scenarios.expected_result: The newly created pipeline run is visible in the reports summary list.

scenarios.title: Handle invalid pipeline run request body
scenarios.description: Test the API behavior when a malformed or empty body is sent to the run endpoint.
scenarios.expected_result: The API returns a 422 Unprocessable Entity error due to schema validation failure.

scenarios.title: Accessing details for a non-existent report ID
scenarios.description: Verify the behavior when requesting a specific report ID that does not exist in the filesystem.
scenarios.expected_result: The API returns a 404 Not Found error with a descriptive message.

scenarios.title: Concurrent pipeline run triggers
scenarios.description: Ensure the system handles multiple rapid POST requests to /api/pipeline/run without ID collision.
scenarios.expected_result: The system generates unique UUIDs for every concurrent request and queues them successfully.

scenarios.title: Empty reports directory state
scenarios.description: Verify the /api/reports endpoint returns an empty list when no report files exist.
preconditions: REPORTS_DIR is empty or does not exist

steps.assertion: Response body is an empty list []

scenarios.expected_result: The API returns 200 OK with an empty array rather than failing.

scenarios.title: Injection probe in pipeline label
scenarios.description: Check if the system handles malicious script tags in the pipeline label field.

steps.assertion: Verify label is treated as plain text or sanitized

scenarios.expected_result: The malicious string is stored safely and not executed when retrieved by the reports endpoint.

scenarios.title: Verify UI Dashboard lists recent reports
scenarios.description: Ensure the reports are visible in the UI dashboard using standard locators.
scenarios.expected_result: The reports dashboard loads successfully and displays the history table.

# SCENARIO_ID: 5f457c69-f399-48be-9a95-8bdb1236afcd
def test_verify_ui_dashboard_lists_recent_reports(page: Page):
    reports_path = "/reports"
    page.goto(f"{BASE_URL}{reports_path}")
    
    # Verify navigation
    expect(page).to_have_url(f"{BASE_URL}{reports_path}")
    
    # Check visibility of key UI elements
    heading = page.get_by_role("heading", name="Reports")
    reports_table = page.get_by_role("table")
    
    expect(heading).to_be_visible()
    expect(reports_table).to_be_visible()
